package repository

import (
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for shared_gorm_db_state_mutated_and_reused_across_requests.
// Rule intent: Code that mutates shared *gorm.DB state and reuses it as if it
// were immutable configuration, causing cross-request state leakage.

// SharedDB is a package-level gorm.DB that gets mutated per request.
// This is the anti-pattern: gorm.DB is not safe for concurrent mutation
// of its query-builder state (Where, Limit, Offset, etc.).
var SharedDB *gorm.DB

// InitDB initializes the shared database connection (this part is fine).
func InitDB(dsn string) error {
	var err error
	SharedDB, err = gorm.Open(nil, &gorm.Config{})
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	return nil
}

// FindUserByTenant mutates the shared DB with Where clauses, leaking state
// to subsequent requests that reuse SharedDB.
func FindUserByTenant(tenantID string) error {
	// Anti-pattern: mutating shared gorm.DB state
	SharedDB = SharedDB.Where("tenant_id = ?", tenantID)
	SharedDB = SharedDB.Limit(10)

	var users []User
	return SharedDB.Find(&users).Error
}

// FindOrdersByStatus further mutates the already-tainted SharedDB.
func FindOrdersByStatus(status string) error {
	// Anti-pattern: SharedDB still has the Where/Limit from FindUserByTenant
	SharedDB = SharedDB.Where("status = ?", status)
	SharedDB = SharedDB.Order("created_at DESC")

	var orders []Order
	return SharedDB.Find(&orders).Error
}

// GetActiveProducts compounds the problem with more mutations.
func GetActiveProducts(category string) error {
	// Anti-pattern: SharedDB accumulates Where clauses across calls
	SharedDB = SharedDB.Where("category = ?", category)
	SharedDB = SharedDB.Where("active = ?", true)

	var products []Product
	return SharedDB.Find(&products).Error
}

type User struct {
	ID       string
	Name     string
	TenantID string
}

type Order struct {
	ID     string
	Status string
}

type Product struct {
	ID       string
	Category string
	Active   bool
}
